metadata.content_note: Production Grade 5 English Batch 1 SkillPackage with full mission flow, voice metadata, and real level banks.

subject: English
skill: Main Idea, Text Structure, and Integrating Information

lesson.story_mission: Join the Main Idea, Text Structure, and Integrating Information studio, where fifth graders solve reading and writing missions with careful evidence.
lesson.objective: I can use main idea, text structure, and integrating information in grade-level texts and writing.
lesson.concept_explanation: Fifth grade learners slow down, name the thinking move, and prove each answer when practicing main idea, text structure, and integrating information. The visual model organizes the important text or writing parts so the answer is based on evidence instead of guessing.
lesson.common_misconception: A learner may choose an answer that sounds familiar but does not match the evidence, structure, or writing goal.
lesson.real_world_connection: These skills help readers discuss books, learn from articles, write for an audience, and explain ideas in every subject.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the task, study the model, find evidence, explain your thinking, and revise if your answer is not fully supported.
lesson.mini_lesson: Use the prompt as a checklist. Identify exactly what it asks, mark evidence or reasons, then answer with clear language.

source_a.text: Cities plant connected strips of flowers so bees and butterflies can move between habitats. These paths add color while helping pollinators find food through the season.

source_b.title: Pollinator Paths Data Note
source_b.text: A related source shows connected flower strips and city habitat with a map: connected flower strips.
